---
title: "`r params$wgbiop_title`"
output:
  word_document:
    fig_caption: true
    fig_height: 10
    fig_width: 10
    reference_docx: "bootstrap/initial/data/wgbiopTemplate.docx"
    toc: false
    keep_md: true
params:
  wgbiop_title: "WGBIOP Report"
  strata: NULL
---


```{r introduction, include = FALSE}

# INTRODUCTION ################################################################

# This markdown documents and integrated scripts analyse biological
# age readings to be included in the WGBIOP master table.
# The output is a .docx template that includes
# the results of the analysis and should be used as a standard for
# reporting of age reading comparisons.

```

```{r chunk_setup, include=FALSE}
# CHUNK SETUPS #################################################################

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
                      message=FALSE, results = 'asis', dpi=400)

```

```{r pander_settings, include = FALSE}

# PANDER OPTIONS ##############################################################

panderOptions('table.split.table', Inf)
panderOptions('keep.trailing.zeros', TRUE)
panderOptions('table.alignment.default', "center")

```



---
title: `r params$wgbiop_title`
---



# General info: Overview of the event (species, stock)

```{r overview_statistics_info}
species_data <- read.csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openfigis/RefData/gh-pages/Eurostat/CL_SPECIES_1_2.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

spp <- unique(substring(ad_long_all$stock, 1, 3)) 

nome <- spp
resultados <- list()

nomes<-nome

# Ciclo for
for (nome in nomes) {
  if (nome == "mis") {
    resultados[[nome]] <- NA
  } else {
    resultados[[nome]] <- subset(species_data, ALPHA3CODE == toupper(nome))
  }
}


if (!is.null(resultados[[1]]) && is.data.frame(resultados[[1]]) && !all(is.na(resultados[[1]]))) {
  scientificname <- resultados[[1]]$SCIENTIFIC_NAME
  sp_name_eng <- resultados[[1]]$FULL_NAME_E
  aphia <- wm_name2id(name = scientificname)
} else {
  scientificname <- NA
  sp_name_eng <- NA
  aphia <- NA
}


overview_statistics_info <-c("ICES stock" =paste(unique(ad_long_all$stock), collapse = ", "), 
"aphiaID"= paste(aphia), "Species scientific name" = paste(scientificname),
"Species common name" = paste(sp_name_eng), "ICES WG" = "", "Age-based model"="")


# Table caption
set.caption('**Table 1.1:** Overview of the species from the event: ICES stock, aphiaID, Species scientific name, Species common name, ICES assessment Working Group, Age-based model (yes/no).')

# Output table
pander(style_table0(overview_statistics_info), missing="")

```




# Overview of the samples and readers

```{r overview_statistics_readers}

# Samples OVERVIEW #####################################################
overview_statistics_readers <- c(
  "N. Individuals" = length(unique(ad_long_all$FishID)),
  "N. Images" = length(unique(ad_long_all$SampleID)),
  "Preparation method" = paste(unique(ad_long_all$prep_method), collapse = ", "),
  "All readers (n)" = length(unique(ad_long_all$reader)),
  "Basic readers (n)" = length(unique(ad_long_all$reader)) - length(unique(ad_long_adv$reader)),
  "Advanced readers (n)" = length(unique(ad_long_adv$reader)),
  "Countries" = paste(unique(ad_long_all$country), collapse = " and ")
)



# Table caption
set.caption('**Table 2.1:** Overview results for all readers: Total number of samples (N. Individuals), total number of images (N. Images), preparation method, number of readers [All readers (n)], number os basic readers [Basic readers (n)], number of advanced readers [Advanced readers (n)] and countries participating on the event.')

# Output table
pander(style_table0(overview_statistics_readers), missing="")

```


```{r sample_overview}

modal_age_range <- range(ad_long_all$modal_age, na.rm = TRUE)
# Table caption
set.caption(paste('**Table 2.2:** Overview of samples used for the exchange event', config$event_id, '. The modal age range for all samples is ', modal_age_range[1], "-", modal_age_range[2], '.', sep=""))

# Output table
pander(style_table0(sample_data_overview), style = "simple")

```


# Overview of the results
## Summary table: All readers and Advanced readers

```{r overview_statistics_all}
overview_statistics_all <-
    c( Data = "All", CV  = cv_tab_all[nrow(cv_tab_all), "all"],
    PA  = pa_tab_all[nrow(pa_tab_all), "total"],
    APE = ape_tab_all[nrow(ape_tab_all), "all"],
    "All readers (n)" =length(unique(ad_long_all$reader)))

# Table caption
set.caption('**Table 3.1:** Overview results: coefficient of variance (CV), percentage of agreement (PA),  average percentage error (APE) for all ages and readers and the number of all readers (Data: All) and advanced readers (Data: Advanced) on the event.')

# Output table
pander(style_table0(overview_statistics_all), missing="")

```



```{r overview_statistics_adv}
overview_statistics_adv <-
    c(Data = "Advanced", CV  = cv_tab_adv[nrow(cv_tab_adv), "all"],
    PA  = pa_tab_adv[nrow(pa_tab_adv), "total"],
    APE = ape_tab_adv[nrow(ape_tab_adv), "all"],
     "Advanced readers (n)" = length(unique(ad_long_adv$reader)))


# Output table
pander(style_table0(overview_statistics_adv), missing="")

```
